slides.title:
Theme Coverage
Visual Regression
slides.subtitle: Targeted regression deck for slide types that were previously outside visual coverage.

slides.title: Workstream Priorities and Initial Owners
slides.strapline: Program structure aligns initiative owners with measurable outcomes and clear sequencing.

columns.heading: Revenue
body: Prioritize retention interventions in top churn cohorts and reprice low-margin contracts.
Establish pipeline conversion governance with weekly owner accountability.

columns.heading: Cost
body: Consolidate duplicative vendors and sequence procurement events by spend category.
Launch zero-based spend reviews for discretionary functions.

columns.heading: Risk
body: Harden controls around privileged access and regulatory reporting deadlines.
Track remediation closure evidence with executive-level escalation thresholds.

columns.heading: Execution
body: Stand up PMO rituals with clear entry and exit criteria for each workstream.
Publish a fortnightly steering committee dashboard for decision support.

slides.title: Diligence Requests and Priority Ranking
slides.strapline: Data requests are prioritized based on decision impact and near-term transaction readiness.

table.title: Priority Requests
table.heading: Request backlog
rows: Monthly customer retention cohorts by segment
Required to validate topline durability assumptions and downside sensitivity.
Contract-level pricing waterfall and discount approvals
Supports margin bridge substantiation and pricing control design.
Historical office lease schedule by location
Confirms non-core fixed cost exposure and relocation flexibility.

insights: High-priority asks directly influence valuation confidence and should be completed before confirmatory work begins.
Medium-priority asks can run in parallel if owners commit to documented weekly delivery milestones.
slides.notes: Source: management data room index and diligence tracker export as of February 2026.

slides.disclaimer: This report has been prepared for discussion purposes only and should be considered together with supporting schedules, management presentations, and diligence workpapers provided under confidentiality restrictions.
